DROP TABLE IF EXISTS [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split];
GO

SELECT tpp.row_id,
       CAST(sp.output_string AS VARCHAR(70)) AS output_string,
       sp.position
INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
FROM   [ecms].[tmp_patient_prep] AS tpp
CROSS APPLY [ecms].[udf_split_string_to_row] (tpp.name_first_middle_clean, ' ') sp;
GO

CREATE CLUSTERED INDEX [idx_row_id] ON [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
(
 [row_id] ASC,
 [position] ASC
)WITH (PAD_INDEX = OFF, STATISTICS_NORECOMPUTE = OFF, SORT_IN_TEMPDB = OFF, DROP_EXISTING = OFF, ONLINE = OFF, ALLOW_ROW_LOCKS = ON, ALLOW_PAGE_LOCKS = ON) ON [Warehouse]
GO
/* 20200801: need to evaluate if FEMALE and MALE should be excluded as well */
INSERT INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] WITH (TABLOCK)
SELECT tns1.row_id,
       tns1.output_string + tns2.output_string as output_string,
       102 AS position
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] tns1
INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] tns2
ON tns1.row_id = tns2.row_id
   AND tns1.position = 1
   AND tns2.position = 2
INNER JOIN
(
    SELECT row_id,
           MAX(position) max_pos
    FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
    GROUP BY row_id
    HAVING MAX(position) between 2 and 3
) tp
ON tns1.row_id = tp.row_id
WHERE tns1.output_string not IN ('GIRL', 'BOY', 'BABY', 'TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN')
UNION ALL
SELECT tns1.row_id,
       tns1.output_string + tns2.output_string + tns3.output_string as output_string,
       103 AS position
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] tns1
INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] tns2
ON tns1.row_id = tns2.row_id
   AND tns1.position = 1
   AND tns2.position = 2
INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] tns3
ON tns1.row_id=tns3.row_id
and tns1.position=1
and tns3.position=3
INNER JOIN
(
    SELECT row_id,
           MAX(position) max_pos
    FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
    GROUP BY row_id
    HAVING MAX(position) =3
) tp
ON tns1.row_id = tp.row_id
WHERE tns1.output_string not IN ('GIRL', 'BOY', 'BABY', 'TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN')
UNION ALL
SELECT tns2.row_id,
       tns2.output_string + tns3.output_string as output_string,
       203 AS position
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] tns2
INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] tns3
ON tns2.row_id = tns3.row_id
   AND tns2.position = 2
   AND tns3.position = 3
INNER JOIN
(
    SELECT row_id,
           MAX(position) max_pos
    FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
    GROUP BY row_id
    HAVING MAX(position) = 3
) tp
ON tns2.row_id = tp.row_id
WHERE tns2.output_string not IN ('GIRL', 'BOY', 'BABY', 'TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN');
GO

DECLARE @p as int=1, @mp as int
select @mp= max(position) from [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] as tnsa where tnsa.position<=100
WHILE @p<@mp
BEGIN
WITH cte (row_id,
         output_string_concat,
         output_string,
         position)
     AS (
     SELECT tnsa.row_id,
            CAST(tnsa.output_string AS VARCHAR(70)),
            CAST(tnsa.output_string AS VARCHAR(70)),
            @p
     FROM   [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] tnsa
   inner join (   SELECT row_id,
           MAX(position) max_pos
    FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
    WHERE position between 4 and 100
    GROUP BY row_id
    ) tp
    on tnsa.row_id=tp.row_id
     WHERE  position=@p AND tnsa.output_string not IN ('GIRL', 'BOY', 'BABY', 'TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN') /*20200801: to avoid extra unecessary combo's of baby and twins, affects about 1740 row_id */
     UNION ALL
     SELECT p.row_id,
            CAST(output_string_concat + p.output_string AS VARCHAR(70)),
            CAST(p.output_string AS VARCHAR(70)),
            p.position
     FROM   cte c
     INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] p
     ON c.row_id = p.row_id
     WHERE  p.position = c.position+1
 AND p.output_string not IN ('GIRL', 'BOY', 'BABY', 'TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN'))
 INSERT INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split] WITH (TABLOCK)
     SELECT row_id, output_string_concat as output_string, (100*@p)+position as position
     FROM   cte where position>@p

SET @p=@p+1
END;
GO

TRUNCATE TABLE   ecms.tmp_patient_gender_original;
GO
/* AM: 2023-04-23: First figure out if M/F are mixed up and fix them */
INSERT INTO ecms.tmp_patient_gender_original
(row_id, 
 name_first, 
 gender, 
 birth_date, 
 gender_clean, 
 gender_clean_pct, 
 gender_freq
)
       SELECT tpp.row_id, 
              tpp.name_first, 
              tpp.gender, 
              tpp.birth_date, 
              gc.gender_clean, 
              gc.gender_clean_pct, 
              gc.gender_freq
       FROM ecms.tmp_patient_prep tpp
            INNER JOIN
       (
           SELECT ref.name_first, 
                  wrng.birth_date,
                  CASE
                      WHEN ref.male_pct >= 95
                      THEN 'M'
                      ELSE 'F'
                  END AS gender_clean,
                  CASE
                      WHEN ref.male_pct >= 95
                      THEN ref.male_pct
                      ELSE female_pct
                  END AS gender_clean_pct, 
                  total AS gender_freq
           FROM
       (
           SELECT name_first, 
                  COUNT(*) AS total, 
                  SUM(CASE
                          WHEN gender = 'M'
                          THEN 1
                          ELSE 0
                      END) * 100 / CAST(COUNT(*) AS DECIMAL(10, 2)) AS male_pct, 
                  100 - SUM(CASE
                                WHEN gender = 'M'
                                THEN 1
                                ELSE 0
                            END) * 100 / CAST(COUNT(*) AS DECIMAL(10, 2)) AS female_pct
           FROM ecms.tmp_patient_prep p
           WHERE gender IN ('M', 'F')
           AND birth_date IS NOT NULL
           AND phn_clean IS NOT NULL
           AND mark IS NULL
           AND name_first IS NOT NULL
           AND name_first NOT LIKE '[A-Z] [A-Z]'
           AND LEN(name_first) >= 2
           AND name_first <> 'SB'
           GROUP BY name_first
       ) ref
       INNER JOIN
       (
           SELECT name_first, 
                  birth_date, 
                  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
                                     WHEN ISNULL(gender, 'U') NOT IN ('M', 'F')
                                     THEN 'U'
                                     ELSE gender
                                 END) gender_cnt
           FROM ecms.tmp_patient_prep p
           WHERE mark IS NULL
                 AND name_first IS NOT NULL
                 AND name_first NOT LIKE '[A-Z] [A-Z]'
                 AND LEN(name_first) >= 2
                 AND name_first_middle_clean <> name_last_clean
                 AND birth_date IS NOT NULL
                 AND CASE
                         WHEN ISNULL(gender, 'U') NOT IN ('M', 'F')
                         THEN 'U'
                         ELSE gender
                     END <> 'U'
           AND phn_clean IS NULL
           GROUP BY name_first, 
                    birth_date
           HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
                                     WHEN ISNULL(gender, 'U') NOT IN ('M', 'F')
                                     THEN 'U'
                                     ELSE gender
                                 END) = 2
       ) wrng ON ref.name_first = wrng.name_first
           WHERE ref.male_pct >= 96
                 OR ref.female_pct >= 96
       ) gc ON tpp.name_first = gc.name_first
               AND tpp.birth_date = gc.birth_date
       WHERE ISNULL(tpp.gender, 'U') <> gc.gender_clean
             AND tpp.mark IS NULL
             AND tpp.name_first IS NOT NULL
             AND tpp.name_first NOT LIKE '[A-Z] [A-Z]'
             AND LEN(tpp.name_first) >= 2
             AND tpp.name_first_middle_clean <> tpp.name_last_clean
             AND tpp.birth_date IS NOT NULL
             AND CASE
                     WHEN ISNULL(tpp.gender, 'U') NOT IN ('M', 'F')
                     THEN 'U'
                     ELSE tpp.gender
                 END <> 'U'
       AND tpp.phn_clean IS NULL;
GO
UPDATE tpp

FROM ecms.tmp_patient_prep tpp
	INNER JOIN ecms.tmp_patient_gender_original gno 
	ON tpp.row_id = gno.row_id;
GO

/* AM: 2023-04-23: Now after that fix, see if we only have U/F or U/M or just U */

INSERT INTO ecms.tmp_patient_gender_original
(row_id, 
 name_first, 
 gender, 
 birth_date, 
 gender_clean, 
 gender_clean_pct, 
 gender_freq
)
       SELECT tpp.row_id, 
              tpp.name_first, 
              tpp.gender, 
              tpp.birth_date, 
              gc.gender_clean, 
              gc.gender_clean_pct, 
              gc.gender_freq
       FROM ecms.tmp_patient_prep tpp
            INNER JOIN
       (
           SELECT ref.name_first, 
                  wrng.birth_date,
                  CASE
                      WHEN ref.male_pct >= 95
                      THEN 'M'
                      ELSE 'F'
                  END AS gender_clean,
                  CASE
                      WHEN ref.male_pct >= 95
                      THEN ref.male_pct
                      ELSE female_pct
                  END AS gender_clean_pct, 
                  total AS gender_freq
           FROM
       (
           SELECT name_first, 
                  COUNT(*) AS total, 
                  SUM(CASE
                          WHEN gender = 'M'
                          THEN 1
                          ELSE 0
                      END) * 100 / CAST(COUNT(*) AS DECIMAL(10, 2)) AS male_pct, 
                  100 - SUM(CASE
                                WHEN gender = 'M'
                                THEN 1
                                ELSE 0
                            END) * 100 / CAST(COUNT(*) AS DECIMAL(10, 2)) AS female_pct
           FROM ecms.tmp_patient_prep p
           WHERE gender IN ('M', 'F')
           AND birth_date IS NOT NULL
           AND phn_clean IS NOT NULL
           AND mark IS NULL
           AND name_first IS NOT NULL
           AND name_first NOT LIKE '[A-Z] [A-Z]'
           AND LEN(name_first) >= 2
           AND name_first <> 'SB'
           GROUP BY name_first
       ) ref
       INNER JOIN
       (
           SELECT name_first, 
                  birth_date, 
                  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
                                     WHEN ISNULL(gender, 'U') NOT IN ('M', 'F')
                                     THEN 'U'
                                     ELSE gender
                                 END) gender_cnt
           FROM ecms.tmp_patient_prep p
           WHERE mark IS NULL
                 AND name_first IS NOT NULL
                 AND name_first NOT LIKE '[A-Z] [A-Z]'
                 AND LEN(name_first) >= 2
                 AND name_first_middle_clean <> name_last_clean
                 AND birth_date IS NOT NULL
                 --and CASE WHEN isnull(gender, 'U') not in ('M','F') THEN 'U' ELSE gender end<>'U'
                 AND phn_clean IS NULL
           GROUP BY name_first, 
                    birth_date
           HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
                                     WHEN ISNULL(gender, 'U') NOT IN ('M', 'F')
                                     THEN 'U'
                                     ELSE gender
                                 END) <= 2
                  AND MAX(CASE
                              WHEN ISNULL(gender, 'U') NOT IN ('M', 'F')
                              THEN 'U'
                              ELSE gender
                          END) = 'U'
       ) wrng ON ref.name_first = wrng.name_first
           WHERE ref.male_pct >= 96
                 OR ref.female_pct >= 96
       ) gc ON tpp.name_first = gc.name_first
               AND tpp.birth_date = gc.birth_date
       WHERE ISNULL(tpp.gender, 'U') <> gc.gender_clean
             AND tpp.mark IS NULL
             AND tpp.name_first IS NOT NULL
             AND tpp.name_first NOT LIKE '[A-Z] [A-Z]'
             AND LEN(tpp.name_first) >= 2
             AND tpp.name_first_middle_clean <> tpp.name_last_clean

   AND p1.ln_output_string = p2.ln_output_string

LEFT OUTER JOIN [ecms].[nickname] p3

   AND p1.fn_output_string <> p2.fn_output_string
LEFT OUTER JOIN [ecms].[nickname] p4
ON p2.fn_output_string = p4.nickname
   AND p1.fn_output_string <> p2.fn_output_string

       AND p1.fn_output_string NOT IN ('GIRL','BOY','BABY','TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN','SINGH', 'KAUR')

-- -------------------------------------------
-- Set the while condition to run at least one loop  
-- AM: 20180330: here we basically check to see if a group is a subgroup of another, and update group ids (aka set_id) 

DECLARE @result_count INT;

DECLARE @loop_count INT;

SET @result_count = 1;

SET @loop_count = 0;

WHILE @result_count <> 0
    BEGIN
        SET @loop_count = @loop_count + 1;
        TRUNCATE TABLE ecms.tmp_patient_transform_driver;
        INSERT INTO ecms.tmp_patient_transform_driver
(phn_clean,
 row_id,
 lower_set_id,
 higher_set_id
)
               SELECT DISTINCT
                      A.phn_clean,
                      A.row_id,
                      A.set_id,
                      B.set_id
               FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] A
               INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] B
               ON A.phn_clean = B.phn_clean
                  AND A.row_id = B.row_id
                  AND A.set_id < B.set_id;
        SET @result_count = @@ROWCOUNT;
        IF @result_count <> 0
            BEGIN
--print 'Found updates in loop ' + str(@loop_count) 
                UPDATE tpcs
                  SET
                      set_id = tptd.lower_set_id
                FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] tpcs
                INNER JOIN ecms.tmp_patient_transform_driver tptd
                ON tpcs.phn_clean = tptd.phn_clean
                   AND tpcs.set_id = tptd.higher_set_id;
            END;
        CONTINUE;
    END;

-- Here we go with the added birthdate party. We will accept the birthdates on the following
-- conditions:
-- * The age of all patients in the full set are 50 years or older (geezer rule)
-- * The range of birth_dates in the full set is <= 5 years
--   (This last group covers a wide range of evils: (DD/MM number switches, one of the birthdates is NULL, MM/DD are the same, but a small variation on YYYY)
-- * Range of birth_dates in the full set > 5 years apart, but MM and DD are the same
-- If none of these criteria is fulfilled, the matches are rejected.  The same DOB checks will also be applied for fuzzy matches
-- 50 years or older for all patients for a given PHN:

-- all birth_dates for the FULL set are 5 years or less apart
UPDATE tps2 
SET    [mark]=4
FROM[ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2] as tps2
INNER JOIN   (SELECT DISTINCT
		  A.phn_clean
		FROM
		  (SELECT
			 phn_clean
			 ,MIN(birth_date) min_dob
			 ,MAX(birth_date) max_dob
		   FROM
			[ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2]
		   WHERE  [mark]=1
		   GROUP  BY phn_clean) A
		WHERE  DATEDIFF (YEAR, A.min_dob, A.max_dob)<=5) B
ON tps2.phn_clean=B.phn_clean
   WHERE [mark]=1;

GO

-- The birthdates are more than 5 years apart but the month and days are exactly the same
-- for all members of the set
UPDATE tps2 
SET    tps2.[mark]=5
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2] as tps2
INNER JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT
		  A.phn_clean
		FROM
		  (SELECT
			 phn_clean
			 ,MIN(birth_date) min_dob
			 ,MAX(birth_date) max_dob
		   FROM
			[ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2]
		   WHERE  [mark]=1
		   GROUP  BY phn_clean) A
		WHERE  DATEDIFF (YEAR, A.min_dob, A.max_dob)>5
		   AND DAY(A.min_dob)=DAY(A.max_dob)
		   AND MONTH(A.min_dob)=MONTH(A.max_dob)) B
on  tps2.phn_clean=B.phn_clean
WHERE tps2.[mark]=1;

FROM[ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2] tps2 INNER JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT

			 phn_clean,
			----AM: 2011-06-08.  MAX(birth_date) max_dob, changed by to MIN(birth_date) If BD of all records needs to be more than 50, thne the minimum has to be 50. Nulls are eliminated by min and max funtion, no need to check for them

-- Fuzzy row scores for full set matches at or above a minimum row score of 70% will be accepted as-is: 
/* AM 20180521: There is an issue here with individual records that are already matched deterministically in
prior step, i.e. altenrate set A, may retrun low row scroes and dont pass the maching in this step, or the stage 700 post processing.
This particularly happens when data sources that carry full names are introduced, such as vital stats. To avoid this issue
we get the max row scroe from the alternate set matches and then compare the minmum of that maximum row.
This adds about 8000 PHN sets as of this wrting on DEV */

/** AM: 2023-06-28: iintorduce parameter for fuzzy **/
DECLARE @var_fzg_fnln_row_score as decimal(5,3)=@0

UPDATE    tpfo
  SET  tpfo.mark = 2
FROM    [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] AS tpfo
INNER JOIN
(
    SELECT phn_clean FROM (
		SELECT fzo.phn_clean, tpas.master_row_id, max(fzo.row_score) as max_row_score /* be careful to get row_score from fuzzy out table*/
    FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] fzo
		INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_alternate_set] tpas
		on fzo.row_id=tpas.row_id and tpas.original_match_code='A' /* not needed as at this point there sohuld be only altenrate set A, but checking anyway*/
    WHERE  fzo.mark = 1
		GROUP by tpas.master_row_id, fzo.phn_clean ) fz
    GROUP BY phn_clean
    HAVING MIN(ROUND(max_row_score, 1)) >= @var_fzg_fnln_row_score
) A
ON tpfo.phn_clean = A.phn_clean
WHERE tpfo.mark = 1;

-- 50 years or older for all patients for a given PHN:
-- (modified to avoid use of count(*) over partition)
-- all birth_dates for the FULL set are 5 years or less apart
UPDATE tpfo 
SET    tpfo.[mark]=4
FROM[ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] as tpfo
inner join (SELECT DISTINCT
		  B.phn_clean
		FROM
		  (SELECT
			 A.phn_clean
			 ,MIN(A.birth_date) min_dob
			 ,MAX(A.birth_date) max_dob
		   FROM
			 [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] A
		   WHERE  A.[mark]=2
		   GROUP  BY A.phn_clean) B
		WHERE  DATEDIFF (YEAR, B.min_dob, B.max_dob)<=5) D
ON tpfo.phn_clean=D.phn_clean
WHERE  tpfo.[mark]=2;
  

-- The birthdates are more than 5 years apart but the month and days are exactly the same
-- for all members of the set
UPDATE tpfo 
SET    tpfo.[mark]=5
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] as tpfo
inner join  (SELECT DISTINCT
		  B.phn_clean
		FROM
		  (SELECT
			 A.phn_clean
			 ,MIN(A.birth_date) min_dob
			 ,MAX(A.birth_date) max_dob
		   FROM
			 [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] A
		   WHERE  A.[mark]=2
		   GROUP  BY A.phn_clean) B
		WHERE  DATEDIFF (YEAR, B.min_dob, B.max_dob)>5
		   AND DAY(min_dob)=DAY(max_dob)
		   AND MONTH(min_dob)=MONTH(max_dob)) D
ON tpfo.phn_clean=D.phn_clean
WHERE  tpfo.[mark]=2;

			 ,MAX(birth_date) max_dob
			 ,max(gender) as max_gender
			 ,min(gender) as min_gender
		   FROM
			 [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output]

SELECT *, cast(null as smallint) as row_nbr INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set]
FROM (
SELECT dupe_count,
       phn_clean,
       row_id,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY phn_clean ORDER BY hit_count DESC,
                                                         row_id) set_id
FROM     ecms.tmp_patient_chain
UNION

       phn_clean,
       matched_row_id,
       DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY phn_clean ORDER BY hit_count DESC,
                                                         row_id) set_id
FROM   ecms.tmp_patient_chain) a
;
		 
	-- -------------------------------------------
	-- Set the while condition to run at least one loop  
	-- AM: 20180330: here we basically check to see if a group is a bugroup of another, and update group ids (aka set_id) 
	DECLARE @result_count int
	DECLARE @loop_count int 
	SET @result_count = 1
	SET @loop_count = 0
	WHILE @result_count <> 0
		BEGIN
		SET @loop_count = @loop_count + 1
		TRUNCATE TABLE [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver];
		INSERT INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver] (phn_clean, row_id, lower_set_id, higher_set_id)
		SELECT DISTINCT A.phn_clean, A.row_id, A.set_id, B.set_id 
		FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] A
		INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] B
		ON A.phn_clean = B.phn_clean AND A.row_id = B.row_id AND
					A.set_id < B.set_id
		SET @result_count = @@ROWCOUNT
		IF @result_count <> 0
			BEGIN
			--print 'Found updates in loop ' + str(@loop_count) 
			UPDATE tpcs 
			SET set_id = tptd.lower_set_id
			FROM[ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] tpcs
			INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver] tptd
			ON tpcs.phn_clean = tptd.phn_clean AND 
						tpcs.set_id = tptd.higher_set_id;   
			END
		CONTINUE	
	END

	;

	SELECT dupe_count, phn_clean, row_id, set_id FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chanied_set_dedup];

	UPDATE tpcs SET row_nbr = A.subset_id

FROM   ecms.tmp_patient_chain) a
;
		 
	-- -------------------------------------------
	-- Set the while condition to run at least one loop  
	-- AM: 20180330: here we basically check to see if a group is a subgroup of another, and update group ids (aka set_id) 
	-- AM: 20200920: There was non-consequential issue in this query below where tpcs.set_id could be updated with more than one value
	-- from the inner query and cause more loops than needed. Fixed it now to always use the min(lower_set_id)
	DECLARE @result_count int
	DECLARE @loop_count int 
	SET @result_count = 1
	SET @loop_count = 0
	WHILE @result_count <> 0
		BEGIN
		SET @loop_count = @loop_count + 1
		TRUNCATE TABLE [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver];
		INSERT INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver] (phn_clean, row_id, lower_set_id, higher_set_id)
		SELECT DISTINCT A.phn_clean, A.row_id, A.set_id, B.set_id 
		FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] A
		INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] B
		ON A.phn_clean = B.phn_clean AND A.row_id = B.row_id AND
					A.set_id < B.set_id
		SET @result_count = @@ROWCOUNT
		IF @result_count <> 0
			BEGIN
			--print 'Found updates in loop ' + str(@loop_count) 
			UPDATE tpcs 
			SET set_id = A.min_lower_set_id
			FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] tpcs
			INNER JOIN (
			select tpcs2.phn_clean, tpcs2.set_id,  MIN(tptd.lower_set_id) as min_lower_set_id
			FROM[ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] tpcs2
			INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver] tptd
			ON tpcs2.phn_clean = tptd.phn_clean AND 
						tpcs2.set_id = tptd.higher_set_id
			GROUP BY tpcs2.phn_clean, tpcs2.set_id) A	
			ON tpcs.phn_clean = A.phn_clean AND 
						tpcs.set_id = A.set_id;   
			END
		CONTINUE	
	END

	SELECT distinct  dupe_count, phn_clean, row_id, set_id FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set]
	--ORDER BY dupe_count, phn_clean, set_id, row_id
	;

	TRUNCATE TABLE [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set];

	SELECT dupe_count, phn_clean, row_id, set_id FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chanied_set_dedup];

	-- generate the new subset_id
	UPDATE tpcs SET row_nbr = A.subset_id
	FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] tpcs
	INNER JOIN

										 (partition by phn_clean
											order by set_id) subset_id

select *,  COUNT(*) OVER(PARTITION BY row_id) AS hit_count
INTO ecms.tmp_patient_chain
from (	 
SELECT p1.dupe_count,
       p1.phn_clean,
       p1.row_id AS row_id,
       p2.row_id AS matched_row_id 
FROM ecms.tmp_patient_name_part p1
inner join (select max(birth_date) max_birth_date, phn_clean from ecms.tmp_patient_name_part    group by phn_clean) mb1
on p1.phn_clean=mb1.phn_clean
INNER JOIN[ecms].[date_variant] AS dv1
    ON isnull(p1.birth_date, mb1.max_birth_date) = dv1.date_original
INNER JOIN ecms.tmp_patient_name_part p2
ON p1.phn_clean = p2.phn_clean
inner join (select max(birth_date) max_birth_date, phn_clean from ecms.tmp_patient_name_part    group by phn_clean) mb2
on p2.phn_clean=mb2.phn_clean
INNER JOIN[ecms].[date_variant] AS dv2
    ON isnull(p2.birth_date, mb2.max_birth_date)  = dv2.date_original
LEFT OUTER JOIN [ecms].[nickname] p3
ON p1.fn_output_string = p3.nickname
   AND p1.fn_output_string <> p2.fn_output_string
LEFT OUTER JOIN [ecms].[nickname] p4
ON p2.fn_output_string = p4.nickname
   AND p1.fn_output_string <> p2.fn_output_string
WHERE p1.row_id < p2.row_id
	    AND ((
	    (p1.gender = p2.gender
        OR isnull(p1.gender, 'U') = 'U'
        OR isnull(p2.gender, 'U') = 'U')
			 AND (p1.fn_output_string = p2.fn_output_string
            OR p1.fn_output_string = p4.name
            OR p2.fn_output_string = p3.name
            OR p1.fn_output_string + p1.ln_output_string = p2.ln_output_string + p2.fn_output_string
            OR p1.ln_output_string + p1.fn_output_string = p2.fn_output_string + p2.ln_output_string
						OR p1.fn_output_string in ('GIRL','BOY','BABY','TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN'/*,'SINGH','KAUR'*/)
            OR p2.fn_output_string in ('GIRL','BOY','BABY','TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN'/*,'SINGH','KAUR'*/)
            OR (p1.ln_output_string = p2.ln_output_string AND (year(p1.birth_date)=year(p2.birth_date)))
            OR ((p1.fz_output_match_key = p2.fz_output_match_key)
                AND ((ROUND(p1.first_name_score, 1) >= 0.4
                      AND ROUND(p2.first_name_score, 1) >= 0.4)
                     AND (ROUND(p1.last_name_score, 1) >= 0.4
                          AND ROUND(p2.last_name_score, 1) >= 0.4))))
			AND (
			--(DATEDIFF(year, p1.birth_date, GETDATE()) >= 50
			--	  AND DATEDIFF(year, p2.birth_date, GETDATE()) >= 50)
				  (ABS(DATEDIFF(year, p1.birth_date, p2.birth_date)) <= 5)
			--	 OR (DAY(p1.birth_date) = DAY(p2.birth_date)
			--		 AND MONTH(p1.birth_date) = MONTH(p2.birth_date)
			--		 AND ABS(DATEDIFF(year, p1.birth_date, p2.birth_date)) > 5)
			--	 OR (DAY(p1.birth_date) = CAST(RIGHT(CAST(YEAR(p2.birth_date) AS VARCHAR(4)), 2) AS INT)
			--		 AND MONTH(p1.birth_date) = MONTH(p2.birth_date))
			--	 OR (DAY(p2.birth_date) = CAST(RIGHT(CAST(YEAR(p1.birth_date) AS VARCHAR(4)), 2) AS INT)
			--		 AND MONTH(p1.birth_date) = MONTH(p2.birth_date))
			--	 OR (p1.birth_date IS NULL AND p2.birth_date IS NOT NULL)
			--	 OR (p1.birth_date IS NOT NULL AND p2.birth_date IS NULL)
			--	 OR 
				OR dv1.date_variant = dv2.date_variant
				 OR ABS(DATEDIFF(DAY, p1.birth_date, p2.birth_date))<32
				 OR ABS(DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CAST(dv1.date_variant as date), TRY_CAST(dv2.date_variant AS date)))<32
				 OR (p1.birth_date<'1940-01-01' and p2.birth_date<'1940-01-01'))
				 )
				 OR (p1.fn_output_string = p2.fn_output_string AND p1.ln_output_string = p2.ln_output_string AND 
					 p1.birth_date=p2.birth_date AND len(p1.fn_output_string)>1 and len(p1.ln_output_string)>1))
--AND p1.mark IS NULL AND p2.mark IS NULL 
GROUP BY p1.dupe_count,
         p1.phn_clean,
         p1.row_id,

INSERT INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_conflict] WITH (TABLOCK) (row_id, matched_row_id,conflict_type)
SELECT DISTINCT
       p1.row_id AS row_id,
       p2.row_id as matched_row_id,CAST('F' as varchar(1)) as conflict_type
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_master_stg1] p1
INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_master_stg1] p2
ON p1.birth_date = p2.birth_date
   AND p1.gender = p2.gender
   AND p1.row_id < p2.row_id
   AND p1.master_row_id <> p2.master_row_id
   AND p1.name_last_clean=p2.name_last_clean
   and p1.name_first_middle_clean=p2.name_first_middle_clean
   LEFT JOIN
     (
     SELECT 
            row_id
     FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
     WHERE (output_string IN ('stillborn', 'newborn', 'baby') /* 20200801: for these three we don't care for the position to handle spelling issues like NEW BORN, STILL BORN, and BAB Y. same doesn't apply for the list below	*/   
            OR (output_string IN ('twin', 'girl', 'boy', 'sb') AND position <= 100) ) 
     EXCEPT
     SELECT 
            row_id
     FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
     WHERE output_string NOT IN ('stillborn', 'newborn', 'baby', 'twin', 'girl', 'boy', 'sb', 'MALE', 'FEMALE', 'NEW',
     'BORN', 'STILL', 'BAB', 'ABY') AND position <= 100 AND LEN(output_string) > 1
     /*20200801: the last few after 'FEMALE' is to cover the issue above, overtime we may have to adjust this */
     ) AS true_bb
	 on p1.row_id=true_bb.row_id
	 where true_bb.row_id is null;
GO   
INSERT INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_conflict] WITH (TABLOCK) (row_id, matched_row_id,conflict_type)
SELECT DISTINCT
       p1.row_id AS row_id,
       p2.row_id as matched_row_id,CAST('P' as varchar(1)) as conflict_type
FROM   ecms.tmp_patient_name_part p1
INNER JOIN ecms.tmp_patient_name_part p2
ON p1.birth_date = p2.birth_date
   AND p1.gender = p2.gender
   AND p1.row_id < p2.row_id
   AND p1.ln_output_string = p2.ln_output_string
   AND p1.master_row_id <> p2.master_row_id
LEFT OUTER JOIN [ecms].[nickname] p3
ON p1.fn_output_string = p3.nickname
   AND p1.fn_output_string <> p2.fn_output_string
LEFT OUTER JOIN [ecms].[nickname] p4
ON p2.fn_output_string = p4.nickname
   AND p1.fn_output_string <> p2.fn_output_string
LEFT OUTER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_conflict] tpc
on p1.row_id=tpc.row_id
and p2.row_id=tpc.matched_row_id
WHERE  (p1.fn_output_string = p2.fn_output_string
        OR p1.fn_output_string = p4.name
        OR p2.fn_output_string = p3.name)
       AND p1.row_id < p2.row_id
       AND p1.fn_output_string NOT IN ('GIRL','BOY','BABY','TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN','SINGH','KAUR')
       AND ((p1.ln_output_string NOT IN ('LA','DE', 'JR', 'ST', 'DA', 'DELA', 'AL', 'EL', 'VAN') and len(p1.ln_output_string)>1) OR p1.name_last_clean=p2.name_last_clean)
			 /* ---- dont need to check for babies in last name AM 2011-06-12
			 AM 20180519: the above condition is to take care of last names that are excluded above or one letter, except when the entire last name is just those and matches the other record*/
	  AND tpc.row_id is null;

 This will help with further fuzzy steps, avoiding exact matches to pollute fuzzy potentials */

BEGIN

/* AM 20180401: Here matching on one letter is allowed  but discouraged,(changed now, not allowed anymore), a bit hackish, duplicate matches in this step could occur and we are trying to avoid them, but some of them

SET    tps2.[mark]=1,

	  tps2.stage_id=90010
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2] as tps2 INNER JOIN 
  (

SELECT   row_id,

         --ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY row_id ORDER BY  set_count DESC,max_fn_len DESC,
                                                       
         --                                               master_row_id) AS row_nbr,
		COUNT(*) OVER(PARTITION BY row_id) AS cnt
FROM
(
    SELECT p1.row_id,
           p2.master_row_id,
           COUNT(DISTINCT p2.fn_output_string) AS set_count,
           MAX(LEN(p1.fn_output_string)+CASE WHEN p2.phn_clean is null then 0 else 10 end) AS max_fn_len
    FROM   ecms.tmp_patient_name_part_1 p1
    INNER JOIN ecms.tmp_patient_name_part_2 p2

       AND p1.fn_output_string <> p2.fn_output_string
    LEFT OUTER JOIN [ecms].[nickname] p4
    ON p2.fn_output_string = p4.nickname
       AND p1.fn_output_string <> p2.fn_output_string
    WHERE  (p1.fn_output_string = p2.fn_output_string
            OR p1.fn_output_string = p4.name
            OR p2.fn_output_string = p3.name)
           --AND p1.row_id <> p2.row_id
           AND p1.fn_output_string NOT IN ('GIRL','BOY','BABY','TWIN','NEWBORN','SB','STILLBORN','SINGH','KAUR')
           AND ((p1.ln_output_string NOT IN ('LA','DE', 'JR', 'ST', 'DA', 'DELA', 'AL', 'EL', 'VAN') and len(p1.ln_output_string)>1) OR p1.name_last_clean=p2.name_last_clean)

           AND p2.conflict_flg IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY p1.row_id,
             p2.master_row_id ) B) A
ON tps2.row_id=A.row_id

WHERE-- A.row_nbr=1 and 
a.cnt=1;

         --   AND p2.conflict_flg IS  NULL

DECLARE @var_fzl_ln_row_score as decimal(5,3)=@0

-- AM: 2023-07-03: Added link to master_row_id, as otherwise we would be bringing duplicates including those with no match at all. Although those would be eliminated later on.
UPDATE tpfo
SET    tpfo.[mark]=1
---select *
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] AS tpfo
INNER JOIN  (SELECT DISTINCT
		  p1.row_id, p1.master_row_id
		FROM
		[ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] p1
		  INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_last_split] sp1
		  on p1.row_id=sp1.row_id
		  INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_last_split] sp2
		  on p1.row_id_ref=sp2.row_id
		WHERE  [mark] IS NULL
		   AND ROUND(row_score, 3)>0.000
		   AND sp1.output_string=sp2.output_string
		   AND ((sp1.output_string NOT IN ( 'LA', -- dont need to check for babies here AM 2011-06-12 
										 'DE', 'JR', 'ST', 'DA',
										 'DELA', 'AL', 'EL', 'VAN' ) AND LEN(sp1.output_string)>1) or p1.name_last_clean=p1.name_last_ref) ) A

/* AM: 20230703: Here we take care of duplicates in a simpler way. Also need to take care of ignore flag, as we are now letting those in ***/
-- First mark anything that has lower scores
update fz 
set fz.mark =null
--select *
from (
select tpfo.*, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY tpfo.row_id ORDER BY tpfo.row_score DESC,tpfo.confidence_score DESC,tpm.conflict_flg) dn 
from  [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] tpfo
inner join ecms.tmp_patient_master_stg1 tpm
on tpfo.row_id_ref=tpm.row_id
where tpfo.mark is not null) fz
where fz.dn>1

--- true conflicts, because if we could have resolved the conflict, we would have done by now in previous deterministic steps. although there are potentially more in step below of deterministic conflicts
update fz 
set fz.mark =null
--select *
from (
select tpfo.*,  COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY tpfo.row_id , tpfo.row_score,tpfo.confidence_score, tpm.conflict_flg) cnt 
from  [ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] tpfo
inner join ecms.tmp_patient_master_stg1 tpm
on tpfo.row_id_ref=tpm.row_id
where tpfo.mark is not null
and tpfo.row_score=1) fz
where fz.cnt>1;
--for those that at least one match is not to conflic recrods, records with conflict removed
update fz 
set fz.mark =null
--select *
from (select tpfo.*, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY tpfo.row_id , tpfo.row_score,tpfo.confidence_score ORDER BY tpm.conflict_flg) dn  from  
[ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] tpfo

inner join (select master_row_id, max(conflict_flg) as conflict_flg from ecms.tmp_patient_master_stg1 group by master_row_id) tpm
on tpfo.master_row_id=tpm.master_row_id
where tpfo.mark is not null  ) fz 
where fz.dn>1 ; 

-- those all matches are to conflicts are removed
update fz 
set fz.mark =null
--select *
from (select tpfo.*, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY tpfo.row_id , tpfo.row_score,tpfo.confidence_score) cnt  from  
[ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] tpfo

inner join (select master_row_id, max(conflict_flg) as conflict_flg from ecms.tmp_patient_master_stg1 group by master_row_id) tpm
on tpfo.master_row_id=tpm.master_row_id
where tpfo.mark is not null 
and tpm.conflict_flg='Y') fz 
where fz.cnt>1 ; 

--- conflicts, but if one part only has one record in total, ignore that
update fz 
set fz.mark =null
--select *
from (select tpfo.*, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY tpfo.row_id , tpfo.row_score,tpfo.confidence_score) cnt, tpm.master_cnt from  
[ecms].[tmp_patient_fz_output] tpfo

inner join (select dup.master_row_id, count(*) as master_cnt from (

 count(*)  OVER (PARTITION BY row_id, master_row_id_dupe_count ) cnt

       p1.row_id AS row_id,

       COUNT(*) OVER(PARTITION BY p1.row_id) AS hit_count

CREATE TABLE ecms.tmp_patient_transform_driver

	 gender      VARCHAR(2),
	 row_id      VARCHAR(22),
 lower_set_id  SMALLINT,
 higher_set_id SMALLINT
);

-- -------------------------------------------
-- Set the while condition to run at least one loop 
DECLARE @result_count INT = 1;
DECLARE @loop_count INT = 0;

WHILE @result_count<>0
  BEGIN
	  SET @loop_count = @loop_count+1

	  TRUNCATE TABLE [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver];

	  INSERT INTO [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver]
				  (birth_date,	gender, row_id, lower_set_id, higher_set_id)
	  SELECT DISTINCT
		A.birth_date
		,A.gender
		,A.row_id
		,A.set_id
		,B.set_id
	  FROM
		[ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] A
		INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] B
	  ON  A.row_id=B.row_id
		 AND A.birth_date=B.birth_date
		 AND A.gender=B.gender
		 AND A.set_id<B.set_id;

	  SET @result_count = @@ROWCOUNT

	  IF @result_count<>0
		BEGIN
			--print 'Found updates in loop ' + str(@loop_count) 
			UPDATE tpcs 
			SET set_id = tptd.lower_set_id
			FROM[ecms].[tmp_patient_chained_set] tpcs
			INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_transform_driver] tptd
			ON  tpcs.birth_date=tptd.birth_date
			   AND tpcs.gender=tptd.gender
			   AND tpcs.set_id=tptd.higher_set_id;
		END

	  CONTINUE
  END

-- Code simplified 2011-04-08 NEB by moving to three levels (A/B/C)
--- AM removed the updates, just deal with inserts
-- All records with blank PHN's that were attached to an existing PHN set in 
-- patient_master_stg1 must also be correctly placed in the alternate_set table
-- to balance out patient counts when the user selects their confidence level (A, B, or C)
-- Basic rules to be followed:
-- * Stay away from attaching to alternate set entries tagged with the conflict_flg
-- * Blank PHN's records attached to a lower match code PHN set in the patient_master table
--   than the original_match_code level being processed in this script will undergo a 2 step process:
--   * First, attempt to attach them to an existing alternate set at the specified original match code level
--   * Any blank PHN's still unattached will be added in as partial A singleton entries
-- * The following outlines the process at each original_match_code level in the alternate set table:   
-- * Alternate set additions for original_match_code = A will include all blank PHN's added to B or C sets in patient_master
-- * Alternate set additions for original_match_code = B will include all blank PHN's added to C sets in patient_master
-- Begin by loading patient_stg2 with all the blank PHN's assigned to match code B or lower sets in patient_master_stg1.  
-- Blank PHN entries attached to full set A or singleton A sets in the patient master, are already accounted for in patient master.  
/* AM: 2018-05-14: Non PHN sets never match at A level to a PHN set or among themselves, the only time a non phn record gets to be A
is either singleton, or prematched. As the result maximum match level at master table
is level B. Hence it does not make sense to take those B's and convert them to A in alternate set matches. So in the next two steps only C matches should be 
considered. See comments on the subsequent steps as well */

/* AM: 20180514: There was a bug in this query that used name_first_middle_clean in the where clause
but the field was not populated. So nothing was returned from here, but this didn't matter as we don't need it anyway
Now the bug is fixed, the field is pouplated, and a new method is used, but this step is disabled anyway, as not needed
we cannot have A level matches of non-phn sets to phn sets even at alternate levels. So in a way, having the bug was useful.
This step is now reused for matching C level non-phn sets to alternate sets.*/

-- -----------------------------------------------------------------
-- Since next process stage is B, don't need the B's as they're already accounted for in
-- patient_master
/* AM: 20180514: not needed anymore
DELETE FROM[ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2]
WHERE  match_code='B';

GO
*/
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Match and process for original match_code B:			 			 
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------
-- Attempt match of all entries to existing alternate sets.  Not going to attempt a 
-- rigourous match - everything that fails to match will simply be added as a singleton A
/* AM 20180527: This step if not carefully done, could create B level matches that are less restrict than C, because in last
step of C matches with PHN we do not consider different genders, but this step used to match on differnt genders, we not try to avoid 
it, but may not be successful as each step below, could be linked to more than one record. We try to fix in the last step, to remove the match
however, subsequent runs may match to the right one and so our matching changes every time we run. So we check in every step the master_row_id
to make sure that exists in the lower level match set C. In the end we do a final check and nullify the remained, which shold not exist anyway.*/
/*First match on 4 keys*/
UPDATE    A
  SET
      A.mark = 1,
      A.master_row_id = B.master_row_id
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2] A
INNER JOIN
(
    SELECT tpas.name_first,
           tpp.name_last_clean,
           tpp.birth_date,
           tpp.gender,
           tpp.row_id,
           tpas.new_match_code,
           tpas.original_match_code,
           tpas.master_row_id,
           tpas.conflict_flg
    FROM   [ecms].[tmp_patient_prep] tpp
    INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_alternate_set] tpas
    ON tpp.row_id = tpas.row_id
) B
ON A.name_last_clean = B.name_last_clean
   AND A.name_first = B.name_first
   AND A.birth_date = B.birth_date
   AND A.gender = B.gender
INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_master_stg1] tpms1
ON A.row_id = tpms1.row_id
LEFT JOIN
(
     SELECT 
            row_id
     FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
     WHERE (output_string IN ('stillborn', 'newborn', 'baby') /* 20200801: for these three we don't care for the position to handle spelling issues like NEW BORN, STILL BORN, and BAB Y. same doesn't apply for the list below	*/   
            OR (output_string IN ('twin', 'girl', 'boy', 'sb') AND position <= 100) ) 
     EXCEPT
     SELECT 
            row_id
     FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
     WHERE output_string NOT IN ('stillborn', 'newborn', 'baby', 'twin', 'girl', 'boy', 'sb', 'MALE', 'FEMALE', 'NEW',
     'BORN', 'STILL', 'BAB', 'ABY') AND position <= 100 AND LEN(output_string) > 1
     /*20200801: the last few after 'FEMALE' is to cover the issue above, overtime we may have to adjust this */

) true_bb
ON A.row_id = true_bb.row_id
WHERE B.original_match_code = 'B'
      AND A.match_code = 'C'
      AND B.conflict_flg IS NULL
      AND true_bb.row_id IS NULL
			AND A.mark IS NULL
			AND B.master_row_id IN (SELECT row_id
                                 FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_master_stg1] tpms2
                                 WHERE  tpms2.master_row_id = tpms1.master_row_id
																 AND tpms2.match_code ='C');

GO
/* AM 20180527: Then U genders with known genders and three keys*/

UPDATE    A
  SET
      A.mark = 1,
      A.master_row_id = B.master_row_id
FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_stg2] A
INNER JOIN
(
    SELECT tpas.name_first,
           tpp.name_last_clean,
           tpp.birth_date,
           tpp.gender,
           tpp.row_id,
           tpas.new_match_code,

    FROM   [ecms].[tmp_patient_prep] tpp
    INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_alternate_set] tpas

) B
ON A.name_last_clean = B.name_last_clean
   AND A.name_first = B.name_first
   AND A.birth_date = B.birth_date
   AND (ISNULL(A.gender, 'U') = 'U'
        AND ISNULL(B.gender, 'U') <> 'U'
        OR ISNULL(A.gender, 'U') <> 'U'
           AND ISNULL(B.gender, 'U') = 'U'
        OR ISNULL(A.gender, 'U') = ISNULL(B.gender, 'U'))
INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_master_stg1] tpms1
ON A.row_id = tpms1.row_id
LEFT JOIN
(
     SELECT 
            row_id
     FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_name_first_middle_split]
     WHERE (output_string IN ('stillborn', 'newborn', 'baby') /* 20200801: for these three we don't care for the position to handle spelling issues like NEW BORN, STILL BORN, and BAB Y. same doesn't apply for the list below	*/   
            OR (output_string IN ('twin', 'girl', 'boy', 'sb') AND position <= 100) )

     WHERE output_string NOT IN ('stillborn', 'newborn', 'baby', 'twin', 'girl', 'boy', 'sb', 'MALE', 'FEMALE', 'NEW',
     'BORN', 'STILL', 'BAB', 'ABY') AND position <= 100 AND LEN(output_string) > 1
     /*20200801: the last few after 'FEMALE' is to cover the issue above, overtime we may have to adjust this */
) true_bb
ON A.row_id = true_bb.row_id
WHERE B.original_match_code = 'B'
      AND A.match_code = 'C'
      AND B.conflict_flg IS NULL
      AND true_bb.row_id IS NULL
      AND A.mark IS NULL
			AND B.master_row_id IN (SELECT row_id
                                 FROM [ecms].[tmp_patient_master_stg1] tpms2
                                 WHERE  tpms2.master_row_id = tpms1.master_row_id
																 AND tpms2.match_code ='C');			;
GO
/* AM 20180527: now only on three keys, ignoring gender all together*/
UPDATE    A
  SET
      A.mark = 1,

INNER JOIN [ecms].[tmp_patient_master_stg1] tpms1

/*  AM 20180527: Now nullify mark and master_row_id, i.e. matching, when master_row_id, is not part of the same C set in the master_stg1 table
